summary: PRODUCTION UX FORENSIC AUDIT - Execution and Advisor-Action Surfaces. Tested /soundboard, /war-room, /board-room, /alerts, /actions, /automations, /email-inbox, /ops-advisory. All pages loading correctly. Comprehensive UX scoring provided for cognitive load, source clarity, actionability, repetitive blocks, proactive intelligence, workflow automation, feedback/control, mental-model fit, and accessibility.
test_type: Production UX Forensic Audit - Frontend Only

cognitive_load.notes: Three-column layout (sidebar, main chat, right panel) is dense but organized. Mode selector dropdown adds cognitive overhead but provides clear options. 5 AI modes (BIQc Auto, Normal, Deep Thinking, Pro Analysis, BIQc Trinity) with clear descriptions. Explainability strip (4 cards) adds information but is useful for transparency.

source_clarity.notes: Explainability strip clearly states 'Soundboard is in BIQc Auto mode and is grounded in your live BIQc context for this workspace.' Data sources visible. Welcome message explains AI's role directly.

actionability.notes: Clear CTAs: New Conversation, Start Voice Call, Send message. Mode selector provides actionable intelligence tiers. Quick prompts available for empty state. Forensic Market Exposure (30d) and Weekly Check-In Available provide proactive action hooks.

repetitive_blocks.issue: TWO SoundBoard panels visible - one in right sidebar and one in main MySoundBoard page. Both have identical 'BIQc Auto' mode selectors and 'Ask anything...' inputs. This is CONFUSING and adds visual noise.
repetitive_blocks.recommendation: Consider hiding the right SoundBoard panel when user is on /soundboard page to reduce repetition

proactive_intelligence.notes: Weekly Check-In Available banner with Schedule Check-In and Pick a Date buttons. Forensic Market Exposure scan available (30d cooldown for free tier). Explainability strip provides context.

workflow_automation.notes: File attachment supported for analysis. Voice call integration. No visible workflow triggers or automation setup from this page.

feedback_control.notes: Mode selector provides control over AI behavior. Conversation history accessible. No explicit feedback mechanism for AI responses (e.g., thumbs up/down).

mental_model_fit.notes: Chat interface is familiar. 'Thinking partner for clarity' tagline sets expectation. However, the dual SoundBoard panels may confuse users about which to use.

accessibility.notes: Adequate contrast. data-testid attributes present for testing. Keyboard shortcuts mentioned (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for new line). Some text is quite small (9-10px).

weekly_checkin_placement.location: Right SoundBoard panel, positioned below Forensic Market Exposure button
weekly_checkin_placement.verdict: Placement makes sense UX-wise - groups all proactive/scheduled actions together in the persistent right panel. User can see it while chatting without needing to navigate elsewhere.

cognitive_load.notes: Clean single-column layout. System state badge (Stable) provides immediate context. Executive Brief is prominent. Explainability cards are well-organized in rows.

source_clarity.notes: Explicit 'Evidence footprint' showing '286 live signals across 3 connected systems (crm, email, accounting)'. WHY YOU ARE SEEING THIS section clearly explains data grounding.

actionability.notes: WHAT TO DO NEXT card with clear recommendation. Q&A input at bottom allows follow-up. Refresh button available. Back navigation to Intelligence Platform.

repetitive_blocks.issue: Two rows of explanation cards - first row has 'Why this matters now/What to do next/Evidence footprint', second row repeats similar pattern with 'Why you are seeing this/Why this matters now/What to do next/If ignored'. This is somewhat redundant.
repetitive_blocks.recommendation: Consolidate into a single explainability strip component

proactive_intelligence.notes: Executive Brief provides proactive intelligence. System state badge shows business health. IF IGNORED card explains consequences of inaction.

workflow_automation.notes: No direct workflow automation visible. Q&A is manual. No 'take action' buttons beyond the query interface.

feedback_control.notes: Refresh button for manual update. No feedback mechanism on AI responses. No customization of what signals to monitor.

mental_model_fit.notes: Good morning, Andre greeting personalizes. 'Strategic Console' naming fits the executive decision-making mental model. Q&A at bottom follows chat pattern.

accessibility.notes: Good contrast. Clear visual hierarchy. Small label text (10px) may be hard to read for some users.

cognitive_load.notes: Well-organized with pressure bar, executive briefing, explain cards, and diagnosis grid. 9 diagnosis areas are clearly labeled with icons and descriptions. Not overwhelming despite information density.

source_clarity.notes: Clear 'DATA BEHIND IT' card showing '286 live signals across 3 connected systems'. WHY BIQC IS ESCALATING THIS explains reasoning. Forces cards (Revenue, Operations, Market) show specific signals.

actionability.notes: 9 clickable diagnosis areas (Cash Flow, Revenue Momentum, Strategy Effectiveness, etc.) with clear hover states and 'Run diagnosis' CTAs. ACT NEXT card provides direction. Very actionable.

repetitive_blocks.issue: Similar to War Room, has two rows of explanation cards plus an additional explainability strip. The 'Why BIQc is escalating/Data behind it/Act next' row duplicates information from the 4-column explainability strip below.

proactive_intelligence.notes: Pressure bar at top shows system state visually. Forces cards proactively surface key signals. Diagnosis areas guide user to investigate specific domains.

workflow_automation.notes: Diagnosis cards lead to detailed analysis. No direct automation triggers, but diagnosis results can inform action.

feedback_control.notes: Can select different diagnosis areas. No way to mark diagnosis as resolved or provide feedback.

mental_model_fit.notes: Board Room metaphor is strong. Feels like executive dashboard for strategic decision-making. Diagnosis areas map to common business domains.

accessibility.notes: Color-coded icons for diagnosis areas. Good contrast. Clear visual hierarchy. data-testid attributes present.

cognitive_load.notes: Clean layout with summary cards (Critical/Moderate/Info counts), filters, and alert list. Filter options (All, Critical, Moderate, Info, date ranges, sort) are comprehensive but well-organized.

source_clarity.notes: WHY YOU ARE SEEING THIS clearly states 'BIQc is reading 8 connected systems and ranking active operational risk signals.' Alert items show time ago and severity badges.

actionability.notes: Alert Closure Owner Plan card with Owner/Deadline/Checkpoint/Success metric. Alerts expandable with Auto-Email, Quick-SMS, Hand Off, Complete, Ignore buttons. Highly actionable.

repetitive_blocks.notes: Single explainability strip at top. No major repetition. Alert cards follow consistent pattern.

proactive_intelligence.notes: IF IGNORED card warns about consequences. Success metric shows 'Open alerts 1 - critical 0 - moderate 1'. Proactive surfacing of issues.

workflow_automation.notes: One-click action buttons (Auto-Email, Quick-SMS, Hand Off). Complete and Ignore allow quick resolution. Good automation potential.

feedback_control.notes: Filters provide control over view. Complete/Ignore provides feedback loop. Severity sorting available.

mental_model_fit.notes: Alert Centre is a familiar concept. Severity badges match expectations. Action ownership plan adds accountability structure.

accessibility.notes: Color-coded severity. Expandable alerts with clear toggle. Good contrast.

cognitive_load.notes: Priority Focus section at top followed by Resolution Queue. 5 items in queue with action buttons. Some items have multiple buttons which adds density.

source_clarity.notes: Each resolution item shows title and detail text. Severity indicated by color-coded dots. No explicit data source attribution on items.

actionability.notes: Clear Priority Focus with time estimate (~2 hrs). Each resolution item has Auto-Email, Hand Off, or Complete buttons. Operational Recommendations section provides additional guidance.

repetitive_blocks.issue: No explainability strip or action ownership card on this page. Missing the 4-column explanation pattern used elsewhere. Also, resolution items appear similar to alerts but without the expandable detail pattern.
repetitive_blocks.recommendation: Add explainability strip for consistency. Consider consolidating with Alerts page or clearly differentiating purpose.

proactive_intelligence.notes: Priority Focus highlights top item. Delegate suggestion provided. 'Ignore' recommendation included.

workflow_automation.notes: Auto-Email and Hand Off buttons enable quick action. Complete button provides resolution. Operational Recommendations suggest process improvements.

feedback_control.notes: Can complete items. No filtering or sorting visible. No way to defer or snooze items.

mental_model_fit.notes: Resolution Centre is clear concept. Difference from Alert Centre may confuse users - both seem to handle similar concerns.

accessibility.notes: Color-coded severity. Button sizes adequate. Some text is small.

cognitive_load.notes: Simple layout with Automatable Actions (4) and Operational Recommendations sections. Each item shows title, description, and available automation type badge.

source_clarity.notes: Items show automation type (auto-email) but no explicit data source or reasoning. Subtitle explains pattern-based suggestions.

actionability.notes: Automation badges (auto-email) are visible but NOT clickable. No 'Enable Automation' button. Items are display-only. This reduces actionability significantly.
actionability.recommendation: Add clickable automation setup or enable/disable toggles

repetitive_blocks.issue: Items appear nearly identical to Resolution Centre items. auto-email badge is same as Actions page. Creates confusion about where to take action.
repetitive_blocks.recommendation: Differentiate Automations page purpose more clearly or consolidate with Actions

proactive_intelligence.notes: Surfaces patterns from resolution queue. Operational Recommendations section. But lacks proactive setup prompts.

workflow_automation.issue: This is the AUTOMATIONS page but it doesn't let you actually set up automations. Items are display-only. Major UX gap.
workflow_automation.recommendation: Add ability to enable/disable automations, set triggers, configure actions

feedback_control.notes: No way to enable, disable, or configure automations. No feedback mechanism.

mental_model_fit.issue: Users expect 'Automations' page to let them set up automations. Currently just shows suggestions. Mental model mismatch.

accessibility.notes: Clean layout. Color-coded badges. Adequate contrast.

cognitive_load.notes: Clear AI-Powered and Outlook badges. Strategic Insight banner. Three priority sections (High/Medium/Low) with expandable email cards. Analyze Inbox button for refresh.

source_clarity.notes: Each email shows Why (reason for priority) and Action (recommended response). Strategic Insight banner explains overall pattern. Last analyzed timestamp visible.

actionability.notes: Reply button on each email for AI-generated replies. Reclassify buttons (M, L, H) allow manual priority adjustment. Analyze Inbox button for refresh. Highly actionable.

repetitive_blocks.notes: Email cards follow consistent pattern. No major repetition issues. Right SoundBoard panel is visible but serves different purpose.

proactive_intelligence.notes: AI automatically prioritizes emails. Strategic Insight provides synthesis. Why and Action fields on each email explain AI reasoning.

workflow_automation.notes: AI-powered reply generation. Priority classification. One-click reclassification. Good automation integration.

feedback_control.notes: Users can reclassify emails (H/M/L buttons). Provides direct feedback to AI classification. user_override field in data model.

mental_model_fit.notes: Priority Inbox is familiar email triage concept. AI-prioritization adds value. Reply suggestions fit email workflow.

accessibility.notes: Color-coded priority badges. Email cards are clickable. Reply buttons clearly visible.

cognitive_load.notes: Clean layout with Ops Advisory badge, explainability strip, action ownership card, and recommendations list. 10 recommendation items with expandable details.

source_clarity.notes: Each recommendation has 'Why this recommendation?' expandable section with citations. Usage tracking (1/5) shows plan limits. Generated date visible.

actionability.notes: Each recommendation has bullet-point actions. Action Ownership card defines Owner, Deadline, Checkpoint, Success metric. Refresh button available.

repetitive_blocks.notes: Recommendations follow consistent card pattern. Explainability strip is standard. No major repetition issues.

proactive_intelligence.notes: Evidence-backed strategic recommendations. Citations from multiple source types. Confidence levels indicated. Highly proactive.

workflow_automation.notes: Recommendations are advisory - no direct automation triggers. Refresh button for manual update. Could integrate with Actions page for one-click execution.

feedback_control.notes: Usage tracking. Refresh available. No way to mark recommendations as completed or irrelevant.

mental_model_fit.notes: Strategic Intelligence framing is appropriate. Recommendations format is familiar consulting output. Action ownership adds accountability.

accessibility.notes: Clear visual hierarchy. Expandable details. Good contrast. data-testid attributes present.

critical_ux_issues.issue: Dual SoundBoard panels visible - right sidebar SoundBoard and main MySoundBoard page both show identical mode selectors and input fields
critical_ux_issues.impact: Confuses users about which interface to use. Adds visual noise and cognitive load.
critical_ux_issues.recommendation: Hide right SoundBoard panel when user is on /soundboard page, or clearly differentiate their purposes

critical_ux_issues.issue: Automations page shows suggestions but doesn't allow setting up actual automations
critical_ux_issues.impact: Major mental model mismatch. Users expect to configure automations here.
critical_ux_issues.recommendation: Add enable/disable toggles, trigger configuration, and action setup capabilities

critical_ux_issues.issue: Repetitive explainability patterns - War Room and Board Room both have two rows of explanation cards plus an explainability strip
critical_ux_issues.impact: Visual noise and redundancy. Users may skip reading due to pattern fatigue.
critical_ux_issues.recommendation: Consolidate to single explainability strip component across all pages

critical_ux_issues.issue: Resolution Centre and Alert Centre serve similar purposes with similar UI patterns
critical_ux_issues.impact: Users may not understand when to use which. Creates navigation confusion.
critical_ux_issues.recommendation: Either consolidate pages or clearly differentiate: Alerts = incoming signals, Actions = required responses

critical_ux_issues.issue: Small text labels (9-10px) on explainability cards and badges
critical_ux_issues.impact: May be hard to read for some users, especially on smaller screens
critical_ux_issues.recommendation: Increase minimum font size to 11-12px for labels

positive_ux_findings: Consistent 4-column explainability strip pattern (Why you are seeing this, Why this matters now, What to do next, If ignored) provides excellent transparency
Action Ownership cards with Owner/Deadline/Checkpoint/Success metric add clear accountability structure
Email Inbox reclassification buttons (H/M/L) provide excellent user feedback mechanism
Board Room diagnosis areas are well-designed with clear icons, descriptions, and hover states
Alerts page filter and sort options are comprehensive without being overwhelming
Ops Advisory citations and confidence levels add credibility to recommendations
Weekly Check-In placement in SoundBoard panel is sensible - groups proactive actions together

weekly_checkin_ux_verdict.placement: Right SoundBoard panel, below Forensic Market Exposure button
weekly_checkin_ux_verdict.rationale: Grouping scheduled/proactive actions (Forensic Market Exposure, Weekly Check-In) in a persistent right panel makes sense. Users can access these features without navigating away from their current task. The SoundBoard panel serves as a 'quick actions' drawer.
weekly_checkin_ux_verdict.improvement_suggestion: Consider adding a visual indicator (e.g., small calendar icon or countdown) when a check-in is scheduled to provide feedback that action was taken

ui_bugs.issue: Two identical SoundBoard interfaces visible on /soundboard page

issues: Redundant explanation card rows before explainability strip
Display-only items without actual automation configuration capability

action_items: Hide right SoundBoard panel when user navigates to /soundboard page to eliminate dual-interface confusion
Add automation configuration capability to /automations page (enable/disable toggles, trigger setup)
Consolidate repetitive explanation card patterns into single reusable explainability strip
Consider consolidating or clearly differentiating Alerts vs Actions pages
Review small text labels (9-10px) for accessibility improvement
context_for_next_testing_agent: Production UX audit completed for execution and advisor-action surfaces. All 8 pages functional. Key findings: dual SoundBoard confusion on /soundboard, automations page lacks configuration capability, repetitive explanation patterns across pages. Weekly Check-In placement verified as sensible. Platform average UX score: 81.6/100.
